ud4.4:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha at the Bamboo Forest, the Squirrels’ Sanctuary.
ud4.4:1.3: And on that occasion Ven. Sāriputta and Ven. Mahā Moggallāna were staying in Pigeon Cave.
ud4.4:1.4:
Then, on a moonlit night, Ven. Sāriputta–his head newly shaven–
was sitting in the open air, having attained a certain level of concentration.
ud4.4:2.1: And on that occasion two yakkhas who were companions were flying from north to south on some business or other.
ud4.4:2.2: They saw Ven. Sāriputta–his head newly shaven–sitting in the open air.
ud4.4:2.3: Seeing him, the first yakkha said to the second,
ud4.4:2.4: “I’m inspired to give this contemplative a blow on the head.”
ud4.4:2.5: When this was said, the second yakkha said to the first,
ud4.4:2.6:
“Enough of that, my good friend.
Don’t lay a hand on the contemplative.
ud4.4:2.7: He’s an outstanding contemplative, of great power & great might.”
ud4.4:3.1: A second time, the first yakkha said to the second,
ud4.4:3.2: “I’m inspired to give this contemplative a blow on the head.”
ud4.4:3.3: A second time, the second yakkha said to the first,
ud4.4:3.4:
“Enough of that, my good friend.
Don’t lay a hand on the contemplative.
ud4.4:3.5: He’s an outstanding contemplative, of great power & great might.”
ud4.4:3.6: A third time, the first yakkha said to the second,
ud4.4:3.7: “I’m inspired to give this contemplative a blow on the head.”
ud4.4:3.8: A third time, the second yakkha said to the first,
ud4.4:3.9:
“Enough of that, my good friend.
Don’t lay a hand on the contemplative.
ud4.4:3.10: He’s an outstanding contemplative, of great power & great might.”
ud4.4:4.1: Then the first yakkha, ignoring the second yakkha, gave Ven. Sāriputta a blow on the head.
ud4.4:4.2: And with that blow he might have knocked over an elephant seven or eight cubits tall, or split a great rocky crag.
ud4.4:4.3:
But right there the yakkha–yelling,
“I’m burning!”–fell into the Great Hell.
ud4.4:5.1:
Now, Ven. Moggallāna–with his divine eye, pure and surpassing the human–
saw the yakkha give Ven. Sāriputta a blow on the head.
ud4.4:5.2: Seeing this, he went to Ven. Sāriputta and, on arrival, said to him,
ud4.4:5.3:
“I hope you are well, friend Sāriputta.
I hope you are comfortable.
I hope you are feeling no pain.”
ud4.4:5.4:
“I am well, friend Moggallāna.
I am comfortable.
ud4.4:5.5: But I do have a slight headache.”
ud4.4:6.3: Just now a yakkha gave you a blow on the head.
ud4.4:6.4:
So great was that blow that he might have knocked over an elephant seven or eight cubits tall,
or split a great rocky crag.
But all you say is this:
ud4.4:6.5:
‘I am well, friend Moggallāna.
I am comfortable.
ud4.4:6.6: But I do have a slight headache’!”
ud4.4:7.3: Where you saw a yakkha just now, I didn’t even see a dust devil!”
ud4.4:8.1:
The Blessed One–with the divine ear-property, pure and surpassing the human–
heard those two great beings conversing in this way.
ud4.4:9.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud4.4:10.3: dispassionate for things that spark passion,
ud4.4:10.4: unprovoked by things that spark provocation:
ud4.4:10.5: When one’s mind is developed like this,
ud4.4:10.6: from where can there come to him suffering & stress?
